subject.name: Plumbing
subject.description: Hand tools, pipework, fittings, hot & cold systems and Water Regs.

quizzes.title: Hand Tool Identification
quizzes.description: Name the tool and know when to reach for it.

questions.text: Which tool is designed specifically for gripping and turning round pipework?
questions.explain: A Stilson has hardened, angled jaws that bite harder as force increases — ideal on round steel pipe. It will chew up chrome or brass fittings, so use an adjustable spanner on those.

questions.text: You need to undo a tap back-nut in the confined space behind a basin. What do you use?
questions.explain: A basin wrench has a long shaft and a swivelling head so it reaches up behind the bowl where no spanner will fit.

questions.text: A pipe slice can be used on copper pipe already fixed close to a wall.
questions.explain: A pipe slice only needs a small rotation clearance, which is why it beats a rotary cutter in tight spots.

questions.text: Select ALL tools you would need to make a soldered capillary joint on 15 mm copper.
questions.explain: Wire strippers and a multimeter are electrical tools — no place on a capillary joint.

questions.text: Put the steps for making an end-feed soldered joint into the correct order.
items: Cut the pipe square and remove the burr
Clean the pipe end and fitting socket
Apply flux to the cleaned surfaces
Heat the joint evenly until the flux sizzles
Feed solder to the mouth until a ring forms
Allow to cool undisturbed, then wipe off flux residue
questions.explain: Cut → clean → flux → assemble → heat → feed → cool. Cleaning after fluxing, or moving the joint while it cools, are the two classic fails.

questions.scenario: You arrive at a customer's house to change a leaking radiator valve. The system is a sealed pressurised system at 1.5 bar. You isolate the valve and start to undo the union nut. Water starts to run out steadily and does not stop.
questions.text: What is the most likely cause?
options: The lockshield valve on the other end has not been closed
The system pressure is too high
questions.explain: Both ends must be isolated. Closing only the wheelhead valve leaves the radiator fed from the return through the open lockshield.

quizzes.title: Fittings & Pipework Systems
quizzes.description: Identify fittings from a drawing and match them to their job.

questions.text: Which labelled fitting is an equal tee?
questions.imageAlt: Four copper fittings labelled A to D: an elbow, a tee, a coupler and a reducer
questions.explain: B has three ports of the same size — an equal tee. A is a 90° elbow, C a straight coupler, D a reducer.

questions.text: Click the reducing fitting on the drawing.
questions.imageAlt: Four copper fittings labelled A to D
questions.explain: The reducer (D) changes from a larger bore to a smaller one in a single fitting.

questions.text: Match each pipe material to its most common use.

pairs.right: Hot & cold water and heating pipework
Above-ground soil stacks in older buildings
Flexible push-fit hot & cold within a building

questions.explain: Blue MDPE is the giveaway for buried potable water. Black MDPE is not approved for drinking water.

questions.text: What is the minimum depth for a buried MDPE cold water service pipe under normal ground?
questions.explain: 750 mm minimum and 1350 mm maximum — deep enough to stay clear of frost, shallow enough to dig out.

questions.text: Drag each component into the correct part of an unvented hot water system.

targets.label: Second line of defence on over-temperature

questions.explain: An unvented cylinder needs a working expansion device plus two independent safety devices (thermal cut-out and T&P relief).

questions.text: A cylinder holds 210 litres. Raising it from 10 °C to 60 °C needs 4.2 kJ per litre per °C. How much energy is required, in megajoules (MJ)?

quizzes.title: Water Regulations Essentials
quizzes.description: Fluid categories, backflow and the rules that keep water wholesome.

questions.text: Under the Water Supply (Water Fittings) Regulations 1999, what fluid category is water in a domestic WC cistern?
questions.explain: A WC is fluid category 5 — the highest risk — because of faecal contamination. It requires a Type AG air gap.

questions.text: A single check valve is acceptable backflow protection against fluid category 3.
questions.explain: Category 3 needs a double check valve. A single check valve only protects to category 2.

questions.text: Which standard covers the design and installation of copper tube for water services?
questions.explain: BS EN 1057 is copper tube. BS 7671 is the Wiring Regulations — a favourite distractor.

questions.text: Maximum permitted temperature at a bath tap outlet to comply with Building Regulations Part G?
questions.explain: Building Regulations Part G3 requires that hot water delivered to a bath does not exceed 48 °C, achieved with an in-line thermostatic mixing valve (TMV2/TMV3).
